pi_items.source: Task setup scripts (setup_task.sh) and install_gretl.sh confirm usa.gdt is loaded and used
Confirmed in export_result.sh and task description
Task description explicitly requires creating log transform of GDP (l_gdp) and using inflation rate (inf)
Task description Step 3 explicitly states 'Perform a Johansen Cointegration Test and estimate a VECM'
Task description Step 3 states 'Set the Cointegration Rank to 1'

pi_items.metadata_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED) via Principles of Econometrics 5th Ed.
pi_items.verified_value: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis (FRED) data embedded in installation script, claimed to be from POE5. Data period: 1984:1-2009:3 (103 quarterly observations)
pi_items.source: Install script references FRED (GDPC96 and CPIAUCSL series). FRED GDPC96 confirmed as Real GDP in billions of chained 2009 dollars (discontinued series). CPIAUCSL confirmed as CPI from BLS.

pi_items.metadata_value: chained 2005 dollars (per embedded data metadata comment: 'Real Gross Domestic Product (billions of chained 2005 dollars...')
pi_items.verified_value: GDPC96 official specification: Billions of Chained 2009 Dollars. Embedded data values (7285.0 in 1984 Q1) do not match FRED GDPC96 official values (7140.597 in 1984 Q1).
pi_items.source: FRED official documentation at https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/GDPC96 and https://fred.stlouisfed.org/data/GDPC96 confirm GDPC96 is in chained 2009 dollars. Direct comparison of embedded data vs FRED values shows ~1-2% discrepancy.

pi_items.verified_value: CPIAUCSL is Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers from U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, monthly seasonally adjusted data
pi_items.source: FRED official documentation confirms CPIAUCSL is the correct series for CPI inflation data

pi_items.verified_value: 103 quarterly observations from 1984 Q1 to 2009 Q3
pi_items.source: Embedded usa.gdt in install_gretl.sh shows startobs='1984:1' endobs='2009:3' with exactly 103 observations

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real macroeconomic data from the usa.gdt dataset containing 103 quarterly observations of US GDP and inflation from 1984 Q1 to 2009 Q3. The GDP data is sourced from Federal Reserve FRED database (series GDPC96) and inflation from CPIAUCSL (Consumer Price Index). However, the embedded data metadata claims the GDP is in chained 2005 dollars, while FRED GDPC96 is officially in chained 2009 dollars. Numerical values in the embedded dataset do not match current FRED GDPC96 values exactly (approximately 1-2% discrepancy), suggesting either historical data from a different vintage or a conversion that was applied. The task correctly requires performing a Vector Error Correction Model (VECM) with variables l_gdp (log-transformed GDP) and inf (inflation rate), with cointegration rank set to 1.
